commands.repair_reason: - [P2] There is no narrow automated repair to queue; maintainers should decide whether the exact-head proof and coalescing identity are acceptable for merge.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern was found; the diff is plugin-local and preserves symlink/path-alias guard failures outside bounded path-mismatch retry. (sha=30688580f1834a59895b987e69de44b2d8ec5bdd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds bounded imported-source `path-mismatch` retry handling and process-local coalescing for concurrent bridge-mode memory-wiki source syncs, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +115, Tests +233. Total +348 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still sends the affected gateway methods through imported-source sync without bridge coalescing, and the PR body supplies exact-head gateway proof for the coalesced path. I did not independently rerun the reporter-scale WSL2/RSS workload in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is no narrow automated repair to queue; maintainers should decide whether the exact-head proof and coalescing identity are acceptable for merge.

target.title: fix(memory-wiki): harden bridge imports under concurrent syncs
target.body:
## Summary

- Add bounded retry handling for transient `FsSafeError: path-mismatch` while writing imported memory-wiki source pages.
- Coalesce concurrent bridge imported-source syncs for the same resolved vault/config so `wiki.status`, `wiki.importInsights`, and `wiki.palace` share the same in-flight bridge sync instead of launching duplicate import/index-refresh passes.
- Preserve existing vault safety boundaries for symlink/path-alias and directory-collision cases.
- Rebase the existing PR onto current `origin/main` and keep main's shared guarded page-write path while retaining this PR's imported-source retry and bridge sync coalescing.
- **What did NOT change:** Out of scope and unchanged: no channel delivery, provider routing, plugin-hook contract, gateway method signature, config schema/default, or broad session model change; the behavior change is only the `extensions/memory-wiki` imported-source retry/coalescing path.
- Why it matters / User impact: concurrent memory-wiki bridge imports no longer duplicate expensive import passes or fail the bridge surface on transient filesystem `path-mismatch` races, without weakening path safety.

Related to #91154.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Bridge-mode memory-wiki imported-source sync runs through the real OpenClaw gateway surface for `wiki.status`, `wiki.importInsights`, and `wiki.palace`; concurrent bridge calls for the same vault/config coalesce to one underlying bridge ingest/index-refresh pass; transient source-page rewrite pressure does not break the gateway or leave the bridge vault inconsistent.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux worktree `/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91828`, exact head `30688580f1834a59895b987e69de44b2d8ec5bdd`, local `origin/main` ref `258b83c4386cdaf2cee0d64af64d20bb09443347`. Runtime used an isolated loopback OpenClaw gateway with `OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr91828-runtime-none/home`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr91828-runtime-none/state`, memory-wiki `vaultMode=bridge`, `bridge.readMemoryArtifacts=true`, and a public `MEMORY.md` artifact in the isolated workspace. Secrets and gateway tokens were not printed; redacted setup metadata is in `runtime-gateway-device-scope-setup-20260624.txt`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd "/media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-91828"
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call health --json --timeout 120000
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call wiki.status --json --timeout 120000
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call wiki.importInsights --json --timeout 120000
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call wiki.palace --json --timeout 120000
# After editing the live workspace MEMORY.md artifact, launch 6 concurrent gateway calls:
#   2 x wiki.status, 2 x wiki.importInsights, 2 x wiki.palace
# Then run 8 wiki.bridge.import calls while an external writer repeatedly replaces the generated bridge source page.
node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway call wiki.noSuchMethod --json --timeout 120000
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Evidence files recorded by daily-fix record-verification on head 30688580f1834a59895b987e69de44b2d8ec5bdd:
- /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91828-evidence/runtime-verification-summary-20260624.md
- /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91828-evidence/runtime-gateway-bridge-artifact-calls-20260624.txt
- /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91828-evidence/runtime-gateway-concurrent-bridge-calls-20260624.txt
- /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91828-evidence/runtime-gateway-path-mismatch-race-attempt-20260624.txt
- /media/vdb/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-91828-evidence/runtime-gateway-negative-probe-20260624.txt

runtime-gateway-bridge-artifact-calls-20260624.txt:
- health: exit=0, memory-core and memory-wiki loaded, plugin errors=[]
- wiki.status: exit=0, vaultMode=bridge, bridgePublicArtifactCount=1, pageCounts.source=1, sourceCounts.bridge=1, warnings=[]
- wiki.importInsights: exit=0, totalItems=0, totalClusters=0, clusters=[]
- wiki.palace: exit=0, totalPages=10, pageCounts.source=1
- generated bridge source page: <runtime-root>/wiki/sources/bridge-workspace-b0b5d6b4-memory-2d62c497.md

runtime-gateway-concurrent-bridge-calls-20260624.txt:
- six concurrent calls all exited 0:
  import_a wiki.importInsights 0 26020
  import_b wiki.importInsights 0 26026
  palace_a wiki.palace 0 26037
  palace_b wiki.palace 0 25994
  status_a wiki.status 0 26131
  status_b wiki.status 0 26146
- ingest_count_before=1, ingest_count_after=2, ingest_delta=1
- new_ingest_2 details: artifactCount=1, updatedCount=1, skippedCount=0, workspaces=1

runtime-gateway-path-mismatch-race-attempt-20260624.txt:
- external writer repeatedly replaced /tmp/openclaw-pr91828-runtime-none/wiki/sources/bridge-workspace-b0b5d6b4-memory-2d62c497.md
- 8 x wiki.bridge.import exited 0
- each import reported artifactCount=1, updatedCount=1, skippedCount=0, indexRefreshReason=import-changed
- final wiki.status: bridgePublicArtifactCount=1, pageCounts.source=1, sourceCounts.bridge=1, warnings=[]

runtime-gateway-path-mismatch-log-search-20260624.txt:
- searched isolated gateway and memory-wiki logs for path-mismatch/FsSafeError/refusal/write retry signals
- match_count=0 in both runtime-gateway-none-20260624.log and wiki/.openclaw-wiki/log.jsonl

runtime-gateway-negative-probe-20260624.txt:
- wiki.noSuchMethod returned structured INVALID_REQUEST / unknown method and exited 1; the gateway remained usable.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `wiki.status`, `wiki.importInsights`, and `wiki.palace` all succeed through the real `openclaw gateway call` surface against bridge-mode memory-wiki with one discovered public memory artifact and one generated bridge source page. After a live workspace artifact update, six concurrent gateway calls produced exactly one new bridge ingest log entry, showing that concurrent callers shared one bridge import/index-refresh pass for the same resolved config identity. The source-page rewrite pressure probe did not break imports or final status, and the adjacent unknown-method probe returned a structured gateway error instead of destabilizing the gateway.
- **What was not tested:** The live race probe did not emit a directly observable `path-mismatch`/`FsSafeError` log signal, so this update does not claim a naturally observed path-mismatch recovery event; it proves the gateway stayed healthy under source-page rewrite pressure and the code-level retry remains bounded to that transient error. I did not rerun the reporter-scale ~1,900-file WSL2/RSS-pressure workload, external channel delivery, or third-party provider/API proof on this head. This gateway proof pass also did not rerun the focused Vitest suite; the fresh proof above is runtime gateway evidence only.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 [P1] exact-head real behavior proof for bridge gateway methods and retry/coalescing behavior:** Addressed for the real gateway surface on exact head `30688580f1834a59895b987e69de44b2d8ec5bdd`. `wiki.status`, `wiki.importInsights`, and `wiki.palace` all succeeded against bridge mode with a real public memory artifact; the concurrent six-call probe produced exactly one new ingest entry. Remaining gap: the live rewrite-pressure probe did not naturally surface a `path-mismatch` log/error signal, so that part is disclosed instead of overstated.
- **RF-002 [P1] live/local bridge proof gap:** Addressed with an isolated loopback OpenClaw gateway, memory-core + memory-wiki loaded, `vaultMode=bridge`, `bridge.readMemoryArtifacts=true`, and `bridgePublicArtifactCount=1` from an actual workspace `MEMORY.md` artifact.
- **RF-003 [P1] session-state / coalescing identity risk:** Addressed by the implementation and runtime probe. The coalescing key includes vault path/mode, bridge/ingest/render/search/context/obsidian config, and agent default/list workspace identity; the runtime concurrent batch updated the live artifact once and produced a single ingest delta across six gateway calls.
- **RF-004 [P1] stale gateway-load / reporter-scale proof:** Disclosed as unavailable in this environment. This update does not claim the reporter's ~1,900-file WSL2/RSS-pressure workload was rerun; it supplies exact-head local gateway proof for the memory-wiki bridge surface instead.
- **RF-005 [maintainer decision] merge-risk / canonical retry acceptance:** Still requires maintainer judgment. This rebase keeps current main's shared `writeGuardedVaultPage` write boundary and keeps this PR focused on imported-source stat/read retry plus bridge import/index-refresh coalescing; maintainers should make the final broad-vs-narrow canonical merge decision.
- **RF-006 [signal] fresh proof / re-review:** This update replaces stale pre-rebase proof with current-head runtime gateway evidence and records it through the daily-fix verification marker. The remaining proof gaps are explicitly listed in `What was not tested` rather than hidden behind local tests.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- ClawSweeper previously flagged overlap with #94443 on transient `path-mismatch` retry behavior.
- This conflict resolution uses the current main shared `writeGuardedVaultPage` retry behavior for the guarded write boundary and keeps this PR focused on imported-source stat/read retry plus bridge import/index-refresh coalescing.
- This avoids restoring a divergent direct write retry path, but it does not override the remaining maintainer choice about broad-vs-narrow canonical merge direction.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/memory-wiki/src/bridge.test.ts`, `extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-sync.test.ts`, `extensions/memory-wiki/src/source-page-shared.test.ts`; fresh maintainer proof for this update is the gateway runtime evidence above, not a test run.
- **Scenario locked in:** coalesced concurrent imported-source sync for identical bridge config, separate coalescing identity when workspace inputs differ, transient imported-source `path-mismatch` retry, preserved human Notes, and preserved symlink/path-alias + directory-collision refusal.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** these tests and the runtime gateway proof exercise the memory-wiki import/sync owner boundary that calls `syncImportedSourcesIfNeeded`; they avoid coupling the guardrail to unrelated channel delivery, provider routing, or UI behavior.

## Origin / follow-up

Existing PR tied to #91154. This update rebases the existing PR onto local `origin/main` ref `258b83c4386cdaf2cee0d64af64d20bb09443347`, resolves the source-page conflict, and revalidates exact head `30688580f1834a59895b987e69de44b2d8ec5bdd` through the local gateway surface.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 session-state`, `extensions: memory-wiki`.
- **Risk explanation:** Behavior change is limited to memory-wiki import/sync coalescing and imported source-page retry; concurrent callers now share one underlying bridge import/index-refresh pass for the same resolved config identity.
- **Why acceptable:** The diff is confined to `extensions/memory-wiki`, coalescing identity includes the bridge artifact-discovery inputs that can change imported source results, and the runtime gateway proof shows repeated/concurrent bridge calls produce one consistent bridge source page and no warnings. Existing symlink/path-alias safety remains outside the retry path.
- **Remaining maintainer decision:** Accept whether this focused imported-source retry/coalescing patch is the desired canonical merge path alongside the broader guarded write behavior already present on main.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** Concurrent bridge imported-source syncs for the same vault/config each performed independent artifact-discovery + import/index-refresh passes, and imported source-page stat/read/write treated transient fs-safe `path-mismatch` races as fatal.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The fix coalesces at the import/sync source boundary, where bridge artifact discovery and index refresh are owned, and adds bounded retry only for the identified transient filesystem condition rather than masking downstream failures or weakening path safety.
- **Fix classification:** Root cause fix
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** Medium-high: the exact-head gateway proof covers the bridge surface and concurrent coalescing behavior; confidence is bounded by the disclosed missing natural `path-mismatch` signal and missing reporter-scale WSL2/RSS workload.
- **Patch quality notes:** No skipped-test or lint suppression claims; no broad refactor. The catch/default paths are narrow guard behavior: `path-mismatch` is rethrown into the bounded retry loop, `not-found`/race reads are treated as no preservable existing notes, and the actual write remains guarded by `writeGuardedVaultPage`, so symlink/path-alias safety is not masked.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** Source-of-truth boundary: `source-sync.ts` and `bridge.ts` own bridge artifact discovery, import, and index refresh; `source-page-shared.ts` owns imported source-page retry; `writeGuardedVaultPage` remains the canonical guarded vault write contract from main. Gateway methods are callers of this owner path, not new contract owners.
